subject: Computer Networking
title: DKIM — Digital Signatures
description: Learn DKIM — Digital Signatures in one minute.

hook.text: SPF checks the server. DKIM checks the message itself. It signs the email so tampering is detectable.

buildup.text: The sending server signs key headers and the body with a private key. A DKIM-Signature header is added.

discovery.text: The receiver fetches the public key from DNS and verifies the signature. If it matches, nothing was altered.

twist.text: DKIM survives forwarding. The signature stays intact because it's tied to content, not the sending IP.

climax.text: Gmail, Outlook, and Yahoo all require DKIM. Without it, your emails may land in the spam folder.

punchline.text: DKIM proves the email is authentic and unmodified. Digital trust.

quiz.question: Where does the receiving server find the DKIM public key?
options: From the sender's SMTP server directly
